items.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.
A phone sitting on a car dashboard, on it is an app with details listed including longitude and latitude.

items.title: A new poll tells us a lot about One Nation's new-found support

children.content: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson smiling at the camera

items.title: Tax windfall from Iran war to be pocketed in federal budget

children.content: The extra tax revenue generated by the Iran war will be saved in its entirety, as the government downplays major cost-of-living relief in next week's federal budget.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers at a podium with flags behind him.

items.title: Iran daily briefing: Details on Iran's 14-point peace plan, and what else you should know now

children.content: Iran gives the US a 30-day deadline on its counteroffer for peace, which does not mention its nuclear ambitions.

image.altText: ABC reporter Laura Tingle, with a world map superimposed behind her

items.title: Cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz says it was attacked as peace negotiations continue

children.content: A cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz says it was attacked by multiple small craft, marking at least two dozen attacks in and around the strait since the Iran war began.

image.altText: An Emirati patrol boat is near a tanker anchored in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: 'Friendships ended': Jewish Australians reveal impact of antisemitism

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will hear from people with lived experience in public sessions beginning in Sydney this week.

items.title: Japan's PM touches down in Canberra for first visit to Australia

children.content: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi has touched down in Canberra for her first visit to Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Sanae Takaichi

items.title: We're headed into an AI fog as the automation arms race begins

children.content: Artificial intelligence might be driving growth for data centre companies, but society is unprepared for the long-term risks posed to the rest of us.

image.altText: a man walks between rows of servers

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

image.altText: Bronte looks down at the camera as she paints

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

image.altText: Close up of a woman who bears the results of being assaulted, with one eye partly closed.

items.title: Interest rates and recession: What to know before tomorrow's RBA decision

children.content: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

articles.title: ASX set to open lower, Wall Street hits new record, oil slips on Strait of Hormuz hope

children.content: The ASX is priced to fall this morning, despite strong corporate earnings propelling the S&P 500 to a new record close. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

children.content: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

items.title: Tenants told 'breathing' contributes to mould in public housing

children.content: Despite unsafe and unusable bathrooms, cockroach infestations eating through wiring and constantly damp carpets, some Tasmanian public housing tenants are having to get the regulator to intervene in order to get solutions.

image.altText: Mould in bottom corner of cupboard

items.title: Contaminants are in your clothes, and brands don't need to tell you

children.content: Despite the rising flood of fast fashion coming into Australia, there's little regulation on hazardous substances in clothes.

image.altText: Dozens of brightly coloured T-shirts hanging off coathangers in a clothing store.

items.title: What can you use your personal leave for?

children.content: People employed on a permanent basis in Australia are entitled to 10 days' paid personal leave a year. So what are the different types of personal leave and when can you use them?

items.title: Emerging Indigenous artists reveal their stories in major showcase

children.content: Revealed, Perth's long-running showcase of Aboriginal art from around Western Australia, allows budding artists to discover their creative side while strengthening their cultural bonds.

image.altText: Woven sculptures of cars displayed on a wall

items.title: Australia's most remote communities losing essential services due to price of fuel

children.content: Cultural obligations and essential services are being impacted by the cost of fuel in Australia's most remote communities.

items.title: As fuel costs soar, cycling advocates argue our priorities are all wrong

children.content: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

image.altText: A man smiles in a bike workshop.

items.title: Piastri makes late charge for Miami GP podium as teen makes more F1 history

children.content: Oscar Piastri says the future is exciting for McLaren after the Australian made a late charge to finish third in the Formula 1 Miami Grand Prix.

image.altText: Third placed Oscar Piastri of Australia and McLaren lifts his trophy on the podium

items.title: The 'crazy idea' that became a world-first medical breakthrough

children.content: Travis Reyes survived a horror military aircraft crash but what came after was a two-year battle from doctors across Australia and the US to save his life.

items.title: Former bosses to face court over fatal mine collapse

children.content: Two former bosses are facing charges over a roof collapse at an underground mine in central Queensland in 2021 that killed a worker and seriously injured another.

items.title: No to high-rise in our suburbs, new Victorian campaign group argues

children.content: Residents' groups across Victoria have formed an alliance ahead of the state election, calling for the community and councils to be given a voice when it comes to approving high-density housing.

image.altText: Three people look at the camera standing on an urban street.

children.content: Victoria's fast-growing West Gippsland region was promised a new hospital by 2028, but work has not started.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a hospital nestled in green space.

children.content: A search and rescue operation is underway for the service members who went missing in mountainous terrain.

items.title: News avoidance on the rise as young people feel afraid and angry

children.content: The news has never been more accessible, yet people are tuning out at record levels. Here is how to strike a balance between keeping informed and looking after yourself.

items.title: Man accused of murdering parents and brother in 'grisly' attack

items.title: What you need to know about Queensland's train station staffing changes

children.content: Queensland Rail says it is moving to a system where staffing is based on "customer needs and patronage", but the changes have been labelled as "extreme" that will make some stations less safe.

image.altText: Queensland Rail suburban train crossing the Merivale Bridge in Brisbane.

items.title: Sister of deceased rock climber Angela Ha says the best way to honour her is to 'live life to the fullest'

children.content: Michelle Ha's older sister Angela died after falling from the Organ Pipes while rock climbing at Arapiles in western Victoria last week.
